channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: 1965 Gibson EB3 Bass in Cherry Refin — $2,172.39
item.description:
$2,172.39 · Excellent · New Kings Road Vintage Guitar Emporium

1965 GIBSON EB3 - CHERRY REFIN. A very nice little Sixties EB bass refinished in cherry at some stage and in clean condition. Missing its pickguard, pick up cover and mutes. Two pots original and two new Gibson pots and a couple of changed parts in the control cavity. Really nice playing straight neck. This will make a superb recording bass, it sounds superb and huge and round through our shop Ampeg. Super light weight at 7.3 pounds our 3.3 kgs. Nut width 43mm. Comes with a gig bag.

Customers please note that returns purchased through PayPal will also incur PayPals fees 
(Approx 4%). Customers outside the EU please note that guitars containing Rosewood are now subject to strict CITES import laws in your country. Now requiring certification and therefore incurring extra charges and longer delivery times. Please do not buy anything from us without consulting us first. Please also note that outside the EU, shipping may be more to your country. Eg: Australia shipping £300. THIS GUITAR IS AVAILABLE TO VIEW AND TRY IN OUR SHOP: NEW KINGS ROAD VINTAGE GUITAR EMPORIUM. WE HAVE A HUGE SELECTION OF VINTAGE AND SECOND HAND GUITARS AND AMPS IN STORE...

item.title: Dean Edge-One 4-String Bass, Black - Used — $209
item.description:
$209 · Very Good · Mill River Music & Guitar Shop

Dean Edge-One 4-String Bass, Black - Used

The Dean Edge-One is a straightforward, no-frills bolt-on bass built around a basswood body, maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, and a pair of soapbar pickups — comfortable to play, well-contoured, and a solid option for someone who needs a working instrument without a lot of fuss. Korean-made, with Dean's asymmetrical 4-bolt neck joint that keeps upper fret access clean all the way up the 24-fret neck. This one dates to around 2008 based on the serial number.
At 9 pounds 7 ounces it's on the heavier side, worth noting for players sensitive to weight. Cosmetically it shows minor dings and scratches consistent with regular play, and a few more noticeable dents on the back of the neck. Nothing structural. A solid basic player for someone who isn't looking for a precious instrument.
Condition is Very Good.
- Make: Dean
- Model: Edge-One
- Year: Circa 2008
- Serial number: E081392
- Country of origin: Korea
- Type: 4-string solid body electric bass
- Finish: Black
- Body: Basswood
- Neck: Maple, bolt-on, C shape
- Fingerboard: Rosewood
- Scale length: 34 inches
- Frets: 24
- Inlays: Abalone dots
- Pickups: 2 soapbar
- Controls: 2 volume, 1 tone
- Bridge: Standard 4-string
- Hardware: Chrome
- Weight: 9 lbs 7 oz
- Condition: Very Good
- Known issues: Few noticeable dents on back of neck
- Modifications: None
- Includes: Nothing
Ships carefully packed and insured for full value.
International buyers are welcome — please contact us for a shipping quote before purchasing.
About Mill River Music
Mill River Music is an independent, human-powered music shop located in downtown Northampton, MA, run by a team of players, enthusiasts, and certified repair technicians. Every piece of used gear we sell is personally inspected, tested, and set up before it ships — we don't list it until we'd be happy to play it ourselves.
As Martin and Taylor certified techs, we hold our guitar work to a high standard, and we're committed to full transparency about the condition of everything we sell. No surprises.
When you buy from Mill River, you're supporting an independent local business that genuinely cares — about gear, about players, and about doing things right. We're here every step of the way. Reach out anytime.

item.title: Squier by Fender USED ​​Classic Vibe Jazz Bass '60s (3CS) — $317.94
item.description:
$317.94 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass!
Featuring excellent playability and single-coil pickups, it offers versatile performance and a dynamic tone.
Featuring a smooth and comfortable radius fingerboard and tight frets, the profiled neck shape is a slim and comfortable C-type.

There are minor scratches, dents, tarnishing on the parts, and rust, but it is in good working order and can be played without any problems.
The truss rod is a bit stiff when tightening, but there is still plenty of room within the normal adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

Weight ≈ 4.18 kg
Accessories: Original soft case

The Classic Vibe '50s Precision Bass is a model that pays tribute to the Precision Bass that was born in the early 1950s.
Equipped with Fender's Alnico split-coil pickups, it delivers authentic Precision Bass tones. Enjoy the playability of a true Precision Bass with a C-shaped neck profile, a 9.5-inch radius fingerboard with narrow-tall frets, and a vintage-style bridge.
The headstock and neck feature a finish inspired by 1950s Precision Basses, exuding dignity and style.

There are some minor scratches, but no major dents, and it is in excellent condition.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

Weight ≈ 3.80 kg
Accessories: Original soft case

item.title: Warwick RockBass Idolmaker 5-string Electric Bass Guitar - Burgundy Blackburst — $1,399
item.description:
$1,399 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Sporting a vintage-inspired carved offset-waist body, the RockBass Idolmaker is a stunning example of Warwick’s dedication to comfortable playability, pro-level performance, and top-tier tone! This bass starts off with a resonant and warm-sounding mahogany body, loaded with two massive MEC Vintage single-coil pickups to deliver old-school thump in spades. The neck is a beautiful strip of maple that’s reinforced with ekanga veneer stripes, then studded with 24 jumbo nickel silver frets in a flat 26-inch radius for maximum playability. Looking for an instrument packed with road-ready reliability? A Tedur Just-a-Nut III, a 2-piece Warwick bridge system, and rock-solid Warwick machine heads ensure that the Idolmaker is easy to dial in and incredibly stable once you do.  


Features:

Offset-waist mahogany body is exceptionally comfortable to hold, both sitting and standing

Maple neck with ekanga veneer stripes for enhanced structural stability

Wenge fingerboard loaded with 24 jumbo nickel-silver frets in a highly playable 26-inch radius

Passive MEC Vintage single-coils pump out a massive low-end thump with clear, present highs

2-piece Warwick bridge system for enhanced sustain and string-to-body resonance

Highly stable Warwick machine heads

Case: Gig bag included

Weight: 11 lb 10 oz 

Condition:

We are an authorized Warwick Dealer. This guitar is in like new condition but is being sold as b-stock at a reduced price without factory warranty. It will arrive set up and ready to play. Please see pictures for the overall condition of the guitar. The actual item for sale is shown in the photographs.

Shipping:

Guaranteed free, fast and safe UPS shipping to lower 48 states. International UPS expedited and USPS Priority shipping available to most countries. We ship our items the same or next business day. All items are professionally packed for safe transit. 


Returns:

We back every guitar we sell with a 14 day return policy. If you do not like the way it feels, plays, looks or sounds upon arrival, just let us know and you can return it. See our shop policy link for full details.

item.title: Warwick RockBass Streamer NT I 5-string Fretless Electric Bass Guitar - Natural — $1,375
item.description:
$1,375 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

When it comes to modern bass guitars, Warwick is an undisputed industry champion with a proven record of innovative and beautiful instruments. This RockBass Streamer NT I 5-string fretless bass features Warwick's full spread of classic features — from choice tonewoods like alder and ebony to active pickups and unique in-house hardware, it's all brought into harmonic unity through passionate craftsmanship and advanced German engineering. This version of the Streamer is Warwick’s premiere RockBass model, featuring a neck-through construction, fully fretless for unmatched musical tone and exceptional 5-string sustain. Discover why Warwick is such a trusted worldwide brand


Features:

5-string fretless bass guitar with sonorous alder body

Maple neck with comfortable “C” profile and a natural finish

Steel reinforced neck-through design enhances tone and sustain

Ebony fingerboard with a 34-inch scale

MEC active J-style pickups with 2-band EQ, blend, and volume controls

Chrome-finished brass nut and bridge deliver rich tone and superior vibrational transfer from strings to body

Fluorescent side markers offer 6–8 minutes of glow in total darkness and are self-charging

Warwick tuners with a stunning 20:01 ratio allow for incredibly precise tuning

Case: Gig bag included

Weight: 8 lb 2 oz

item.title: Warwick RockBass Vampyre 4 String Electric Bass Guitar - Black — $1,215
item.description:
$1,215 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

The Warwick Rockbass Vampyre 4-string electric bass guitar is beautifully made and has that famous Warwick low growl. It features an alder body”a solid foundation to provide both thundering lows and distinct highs in an instrument that is just as much fun to play as it is to stare at. The Warwick bass features other premium woods in its design, like a maple neck and a rosewood fingerboard. The 34" long scale, Just-A-Nut III, and 20" fingerboard radius provides added comfort for intricate bass movements.

The Warwick Vampyre's 2 active MEC pickups are paired with active 2-way electronic controls to provide plenty of tones suited for stage or studio. Four control knobs (volume, bass, balance, and tone controls) provide vast sonic range perfect for the studio and the stage. Other desirable features include Warwick machine heads, 2-piece bridge, Warwick security locks, and chrome hardware.


Features:

Warwick RockBass Vampyre

4-string electric bass

34" long scale

2-piece solid Alder body

3-piece laminated Maple neck with black veneer stripes

Two carbon bars for neck reinforcement, from 12th to 24th fret

2-way trussrod

Bolt-on construction

Wenge fingerboard

20" radius

24 jumbo titanium-brass alloy frets (width: 2.9 mm / height: 1.4 mm)

Ball end frets with IFT (Invisible Fret Technology)

Warwick Just-A-Nut III Tedur

Nut width 38.5 mm / 1.5"

Active MEC Jazz / Twin Jazz pickups

Active 2-band electronics

Controls for Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass

Copper shielded electronic compartment, pickup routings with shielding paint

Warwick quick access truss rod and electronics compartment cover

Threaded inserts for pickup screws

Warwick machine heads

2-piece Warwick 3D bridge

string spacing 19 mm / 3/4"

Distance E to G string 57 mm / 2 1/4"

Warwick S-Security Locks

Warwick RED Strings (42200 M) .045"-.105"

Solid Black High Polish finish

Chrome hardware

Case: Gig bag included

Weight: 9 lb 2 oz 

Condition:

item.title: Warwick RockBass Star Bass 5-string Hollowbody Electric Bass - Solid Crème White — $1,380
item.description:
$1,380 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Looking to add some vintage-style extended-range thump to your tonal toolbox? The Warwick RockBass StarBass 5-string is a hollowbody monster that's brimming with warm, punchy low-end power! This bass boasts a fully hollow maple body that lends your tone a crisp, airy feel, bolstered by two huge MEC Passive Vintage single-coil pickups for unmatched articulation and a cavernous depth. Apart from the lightweight hollow body, the StarBass 5-string’s 34-inch scale, comfortable “C” shape maple neck, and wenge fingerboard’s Extra-high Jumbo frets offer stage-ready performance. Picky about your setup? The adjustable Just A Nut III allows you to precisely dial in your nut action without the need for filing or modification!

Features:

Vintage vibe with a modern extended-range 5-string design

Fully hollow maple body gives your low-end tone a crisp bite and unmatched articulation

Maple neck with a comfortable “C” shape

Wenge fingerboard loaded with ultra-high-performance Extra-high Jumbo frets

Standard 34-inch scale for a punchy, muscular tone

Passive MEC Vintage offer a bright, clear tone with a deep bass response

Just A Nut III allows you to dial in your nut action with no modification

Reliable Warwick 20:01 tuners

Rock-solid Warwick 3D 2-piece bridge

Case: Gig bag included

Weight: 8 lb 8 oz

item.description:
$1,315 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Get your thumping, heart-pumping bass career started on another note with the Warwick RockBass Corvette Basic Bass Guitar. With Warwick’s years of experience crafting high-quality, tour-tested, growling basses, the Corvette Basic brings honor to the RockBass family line with these same features in droves. Apart from its stunning design, this alder-body monster boasts some killer tone thanks to its MEC Active J-style bass pickups perched at the neck and bridge. The RockBass Corvette Basic offers some excellent tone-shaping capabilities thanks to balance, bass, and treble knobs.

item.description:
$1,155 · B-Stock · Rayhill Audio

Warwick has been blessing the low end of the mix with its high quality, well crafted, growling tones for years. The Corvette Basic 4-String bass brings to life the quality and excellence you've grown to expect from the Warwick Rockbass series line.

The Corvetter Basic features a long-scale alder body, a bolt-on 3-piece laminated maple with ekanga veneer stripes and a wenge fingerboard. The monstrous tone comes raging from its active 2-band preamp and active MEC "Dynamic Correction" J/J pick up set. Round out your sound with all of the tone-shaping capabilities provided by the Volume, Balance, Treble and Bass Knobs.

item.title: Warwick Rockbass Streamer Standard 5 String Electric Bass Guitar - Ocean Blue — $1,215
item.description:
$1,215 · Mint · Rayhill Audio

Warwick RockBass is like a Best of Collection of our most popular bass models, re-released as standard versions for a wider audience. However, they are full-fledged Warwick basses for demanding players, made of selected tonewoods and featuring high-quality equipment such as our famous 2-part bridge, the Just-a-Nut neck construction, or MEC pickups. The attractive pricing of these instruments is possible because they are cost-efficiently produced in closely cooperating partner workshops in China. At our German location in Markneukirchen, every RockBass is checked and set-up to guarantee highest quality standards.


Features:

5-string electric bass
34" long scale
Arched Carolina body
4-piece laminated maple neck with Ekanga veneer stripes
Bolt-on construction
Wenge fingerboard
26" radius
24 titanium-brass alloy jumbo frets
Ball end frets /IFT (Invisible Fret Technology)
Just a Nut III Tedur
Nut width 45.0 mm / 1.8"
Passive MEC vintage humbucker pickups
Active Rockbass 2-band electronics
Controls for Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass
Copper shielded electronic compartment
Warwick quick access compartment cover
Warwick machine heads
2-piece Warwick bridge
String spacing 16.5 mm / 21/32"
Distance B to G string 66 mm / 2 19/32"

item.title: Ovation B778TX-5 Pro Series Elite TX Mid Depth Maple Neck 4-String Acoustic-Electric Bass Guitar — $1,129
item.description:
$1,129 · B-Stock · The Gator Tone

Ovations Mod TX 4-String Bass features a solid spruce top with scalloped X-bracing for superior note definition. Its classic mid-depth, cutaway, composite body delivers a balanced acoustic tone and is comfortable to hold and play. Multi-port soundholes on the bass-string side of the instrument enhance lower-frequency projection. 

This bass also features a satin-finished maple neck and a black-stained rosewood fretboard. A full 34-scale delivers harmonically rich timbres across the instrument's entire range. With its onboard compressor, both the OP-Pro preamp and the OCP-1K pickup provide control over the attack and sustain of every note.

Specifications:

  • Solid A-grade Sitka Spruce top.
  • Scalloped X-bracing.
  • Multi-soundhole (bass side).
  • Mid-depth Lyrachord GS body.
  • Maple neck, thin profile.
  • Wenge fingerboard, Walnut bridge.
  • Scale 864 mm, nut width 42,8 mm.
  • Ovation machine heads, diecast, black.
  • OCP-1K pickup, OP-Pro preamp with tuner.
  • Original strings ex-factory: ADAMAS NUovA COATED Phosphor-Bronze 5300NU-M Medium .045-.105, GEWA Art.-Nr. 669.615.

Special Note B-Stock:

  • This item is considered "B-Stock."
  • B-Stock items are new instruments that did not get an A+ in QC.
  • B-Stock is the closest to a new condition. There are very small blemishes categorized as “an inch or less”. Many times, these instruments are like new.
  • They usually have minor cosmetic blemishes; none of these issues will affect overall playability.

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX21 VJ5 SGM/R 4.695kg #J000371-25【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,449
item.description:
$1,449 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)


Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.


While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.


This is the MX21 VJ5, a 5-string model adopting the classic JB style.


The body is made of solid Okoume, and the neck and fingerboard are made of Roasted Maple.


This is a standard combination in the MX series.


The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.


The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.


It is equipped with Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style pickups. This JB-type bass boasts a sharp, crisp attack while maintaining a clear sound with noise suppressed by hum cancellation.


The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.


Bass and treble frequencies can be significantly boosted with simple controls.


The preamp can also be bypassed via a push/pull knob.


The preamp's circuit board is almost identical to that of top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.


Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.


These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.


Even at this price point, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising dedication to instrument making is evident.


While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, its sound is truly that of a refined JB.


This bass delivers a modern tone that stands out in an ensemble, with rich harmonics, crisp highs perfect for slap bass, and a tight, well-tuned Low-B note that blends seamlessly.


Based on the traditional JB bass, this instrument incorporates modern elements, reflecting Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.


⚪︎SPECS


Scale length: 34"" (864 mm)

Construction: Bolt-On

Body Wood: Okoume

Neck Wood: Roasted Maple

Fingerboard: Roasted Maple

Fingerboard Radius: 12""

Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III

Nut Width: 1.875"" (47.6 mm)

Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094"" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051"" (1.3 mm))

Neck Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style

Bridge Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style in 60s position

Electronics: Sadowsky Active 2-Way

Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass (Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp (Bypass)

Tuners: Sadowsky Light machine heads with open gear

Security Locks: Sadowsky S-style

Bridge System: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release

Hardware Color: Chrome

Strings: 045 - 135 Stainless Steel

Included Accessories: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector and Hex Key Tools

Gig Bag: Sadowsky Portabag (SAC BAG BASS PORTA)


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Genuine gig bag, wrench, warranty card


Text: Tomoki Hayakawa


Inspection 담당: Kubota


Product Photos: Tei


Manufacturer's warranty included

item.title: 【new】Aria Pro II / SB-CLB - Cliff Burton Signature 4.805kg #W126040137【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,153
item.description:
$3,153 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Aria Pro II is an international brand that has been active since the early days of guitar making in Japan.


This model is the SB, a flagship model of Aria Pro II.


One of the greatest masterpieces in the history of Japanese guitars has made a comeback.


The SB has been loved by musicians all over the world.


Marcus Miller, Will Lee, Jack Bruce, and John Taylor are known bassists who have used the SB. (Titles omitted)


Currently, the latest model, the SB-1000, is being manufactured.


This model is a signature model of Cliff Burton, the bassist of Metallica.


He was a big fan of this SB series.


Its iconic look and sound will be reproduced in a limited production run in 2026.


It features a neck-through construction, with a 7-piece laminated neck made of maple and walnut.


The beauty of the wood grain, which runs smoothly from the tip of the headstock to the bottom of the bridge, is exceptional.


The fingerboard is rosewood. The standard model uses ebony.


The body is made of ash.


The body shape is designed with weight balance in mind; in fact, the bridge side of the body is thicker than the headstock side.


This shifts the weight balance towards the bridge, resulting in a comfortable balance during playing.


The color is, of course, all black.


The tuning machines are GOTOH GB707, and the bridge is a QHB-1BR.


While the standard model's bridge is also made by GOTOH, this likely recreates the look of the original.


This shows that this is not just a limited edition model with a different color, but a project made with love.


The pickups are dual-coil MB-10E.


The preamp is the familiar B.B. Circuit.


This circuit, which has captivated many bassists, is an active low-pass filter that allows you to freely switch between six different characteristics.


From a warm and thick sound to a bright and attacky sound, the character changes with the switch. Simple yet highly practical, it allows for quick and optimal sound shaping to suit playing style and ensemble.


This filter can be switched on and off with a mini-switch.


Controls include volume, tone, mode selector, and B.B. Circuit bypass switch.


It features a thick, attack-oriented sound with a tight yet smoothly extending sustain.


This is Cliff Burton's SB.


SPECS


Body: Ash

Neck: Maple + Walnut, Neck-Through body, Heel-Less Cutaway

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Frets: 24F

Scale: 864mm

Pickups: MB-10E Dual Coil

Controls: Volume, Tone, 6-position PU Attack Mode Selector SW, Bypass SW, Battery LED

Bridge: QHB-1BR

Hardware: Chrome

Finish: BK (Black)


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Gig bag, poster, warranty card


Text: Ryoma Terao


Inspection 담당: Higashie


Product photos: Higashie


Manufacturer's warranty included


◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information


●Lifetime Free Maintenance


Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free ""String Vibration Setup"" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)


・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.


・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.


・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Defect Warranty


Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.


The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.


Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:


* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.


* Replacement of consumables.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

item.title: Surine Quest V 2015 — $4,150
item.description:
$4,150 · Excellent · Kdre's Boutique

Up for sell is a custom Surine Quest 5 string, this bass is an amazing instrument that sounds absolutely amazing! All Bartolini with series/parallel switches for both pickups. The Burl top and back with mahogany core is one of the best sounding Surines wood combinations you’ll ever hear. I’ve owned at least 7 Surine basses! The Certificate of Authenticity shows the retail price of this bass. I upgraded the bass with switches and knobs! You can contact me at (901) 870-1089 with any questions.

item.title: Warwick Custom Shop 2015 Master Built Streamer Stage I 5string — $3,552.73
item.description:
$3,552.73 · Very Good · TC GAKKI

Welcome to TC Gakki! All photos are of the item for sale. Please note they are for sale in our store and may sell out. Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask any questions!

Product Details
________________________________

The pinnacle of the German Custom Shop. A Masterbuilt Streamer Stage I, crafted from carefully selected wenge wood!

Founded in 1982 by Hans-Peter Wilfer, Warwick has grown into a manufacturer beloved by musicians of all levels, both professional and amateur, for its models that embody German spirit.

The Warwick Custom Shop Masterbuilt series is the highest-end line, manufactured in Germany and using even more carefully selected tonewoods.

The Streamer Stage, a best-selling model since the brand's inception, features an arched body that naturally conforms to the body and a neck-through construction.

This particular 2015 model is a 5-string Streamer Stage I with a PJ pickup configuration.

The body is constructed with a wenge top and mahogany back, and features a wenge neck and wenge fingerboard.

It is equipped with PJ-configured MEC pickups, and controls include a master volume, pickup balancer, and 3-band EQ.

This instrument is characterized by a sound that is both full and sharp.
With Warwick's signature clear tone, it faithfully reproduces picking nuances, and depending on the settings, it has the versatility to handle everything from delicate clean tones to heavy rock sounds.

It boasts a hard yet growling low-to-mid range and a dense, crisp high range.
The full-bodied sound provides excellent string separation even when playing chords in the high positions, expanding the possibilities for solo bass playing.

【SPEC】
BODY: Wenge
NECK: Wenge
FINGERBOARD: Wenge
P.U: MEC P/J
CONTROL: Vol/Bal/
WEIGHT: 5.00kg
SCALE: 34”
NUT WIDTH: 45mm
w/Gig Bag

*Neck and electronics have been maintained by our repair technician and are in good condition.
*String height is set to approximately 1.6mm at the 12th fret.
*Parts have been disassembled and cleaned.

[Repair/Modification History/Replacement Parts/External Condition, etc.]

item.title: 【new】Aria Pro II / SB-CLB - Cliff Burton Signature 4.480kg #W126040109【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,153
item.description:
$3,153 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Aria Pro II is an international brand that has been active since the early days of guitar making in Japan.


This model is the SB, a flagship model of Aria Pro II.


One of the greatest masterpieces in the history of Japanese guitars has made a comeback.


The SB has been loved by musicians all over the world.


Marcus Miller, Will Lee, Jack Bruce, and John Taylor are known bassists who have used the SB. (Titles omitted)


Currently, the latest model, the SB-1000, is being manufactured.


This model is a signature model of Cliff Burton, the bassist of Metallica.


He was a big fan of this SB series.


Its iconic look and sound will be reproduced in a limited production run in 2026.


It features a neck-through construction, with a 7-piece laminated neck made of maple and walnut.


The beauty of the wood grain, which runs smoothly from the tip of the headstock to the bottom of the bridge, is exceptional.


The fingerboard is rosewood. The standard model uses ebony.


The body is made of ash.


The body shape is designed with weight balance in mind; in fact, the bridge side of the body is thicker than the headstock side.


This shifts the weight balance towards the bridge, resulting in a comfortable balance during playing.


The color is, of course, all black.


The tuning machines are GOTOH GB707, and the bridge is a QHB-1BR.


While the standard model's bridge is also made by GOTOH, this likely recreates the look of the original.


This shows that this is not just a limited edition model with a different color, but a project made with love.


The pickups are dual-coil MB-10E.


The preamp is the familiar B.B. Circuit.


This circuit, which has captivated many bassists, is an active low-pass filter that allows you to freely switch between six different characteristics.


From a warm and thick sound to a bright and attacky sound, the character changes with the switch. Simple yet highly practical, it allows for quick and optimal sound shaping to suit playing style and ensemble.


This filter can be switched on and off with a mini-switch.


Controls include volume, tone, mode selector, and B.B. Circuit bypass switch.


It features a thick, attack-oriented sound with a tight yet smoothly extending sustain.


This is Cliff Burton's SB.


SPECS


Body: Ash

Neck: Maple + Walnut, Neck-Through body, Heel-Less Cutaway

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Frets: 24F

Scale: 864mm

Pickups: MB-10E Dual Coil

Controls: Volume, Tone, 6-position PU Attack Mode Selector SW, Bypass SW, Battery LED

Bridge: QHB-1BR

Hardware: Chrome

Finish: BK (Black)


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Gig bag, poster, warranty card


Text: Ryoma Terao


Inspection 담당: Higashie


Product photos: Higashie

item.title: 【used】FGN / JMP2B-ASH-M/OPB 3.885kg #K240076【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,459
item.description:
$1,459 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

FGN (Fujigen) is an original brand developed by Fujigen, a guitar factory that Japan proudly presents to the world.


For many years, Fujigen has handled OEM manufacturing for numerous famous brands such as Fender, Ibanez, and G&L, and its high level of manufacturing technology is highly regarded by musicians around the world.


This model is the JMP2B-ASH-M/OPB from Fujigen's J-Standard series.


The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.


It is painted in Open Pore Black, which gives it a distinctive look.


The frets are installed using a technology called the Circle Fretting System (CFS).


This is because the frets are installed in a fan shape, which corrects the spacing between the nut and frets and improves pitch accuracy.


It is equipped with two Seymour Duncan Quarter-Pound SPB-3 pickups.


With powerful output and a thick low end, the mix of the two pickups creates a truly unique sound character, combining the clarity and definition reminiscent of a Jazz Bass.


While maintaining the powerful sound characteristic of a PB, this FGN guitar boasts a wide range of tonal possibilities thanks to its two split-coil pickups and high playability, resulting in a highly refined instrument.


Fret life is approximately 70%.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and finish are generally clean.


The electronics are in good condition.


No obvious modifications were observed.


Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone

Nut width: 40.2mm

Bridge string spacing: 19.0mm

Scale length: 34 inches

item.description:
$1,741 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Ibanez is a leading Japanese guitar brand, globally renowned alongside Fender and Gibson.


It enjoys the strong trust of professional musicians, with Gary Willis, Fieldy (Korn), and Thudercat among those known to use Ibanez products. (Titles omitted)


This model is the BTB1935, a 5-string bass with a 35-inch scale.


The BTB series is a popular Ibanez series with a design philosophy optimized for multi-string basses.


It achieves both high playability and excellent sound balance, resulting in a natural playing feel despite being a multi-string bass.


The 35-inch scale and through-neck construction provide tight response and excellent sustain, even down to the lower strings.


It possesses a high level of clarity and stability, which are essential for multi-string basses.


The body is constructed with African Mahogany for the wings and Curly Maple and Walnut for the top and back.


The neck features a 7-piece laminated through-neck construction using Panga Panga and Purpleheart, and incorporates Graphite Reinforcement Rods.


This provides high rigidity and excellent neck stability.


The fingerboard uses Bound Panga Panga.


This contributes to a hard, fast attack and a well-defined sound.


It is equipped with two Aguilar DCB Humbucker pickups.


The preamp features an Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-Band EQ, allowing for a wide range of sound shaping.


Combining a robust and powerful low end with clear mid-highs, this is a highly refined instrument that can handle a wide range of styles, from fingerstyle to slap and technical playing.


The frets have approximately 90% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show some minor scratches, but overall it is clean.


The electronics are in good condition. No obvious modifications were observed.

item.title: S. Martyn Element Headless 32” scale 18mm spacing 5 strings 7.5lbs - SATIN IMBUIA PREMIUM FRETTING MASTERBUILT 2026 — $3,690
item.description:
$3,690 · Brand New · Bass and Soul Music Gears

*HAND MADE IN BRAZIL🇧🇷

Built by one of the most talented and dedicated Luthier in Brazil with hand picked and exotic Brazilian woods.

This bass is located in BRAZIL, shipping is included by the buyer is responsible for import fees (in US about $160.


The voice:

Fast and articulate up front, with a warmth underneath that keeps notes blooming instead of dying flat. The cinamomo body is a dense Brazilian hardwood with real snap, think of it living in maple's neighborhood, with attack and clarity that slap players will feel immediately. The Imbuia top (often called Brazilian walnut) rounds that edge with a warm, focused sweetness, and it's a stunner to look at.

The 5-piece louro pardo and maple neck gives you stiffness, stability, and a punchy, even response across all five strings.

And the fingerboard is the crown jewel: genuine Brazilian rosewood (Jacarandá da Bahia) the holygrail boardwood. Deep fundamentals, bell like highs, and that famous bloom you just don't get from Indian rosewood or pau ferro.

At 32" scale with 18mm spacing, it plays fast but never cramped: Comfortable for slap, wide enough for aggressive fingerstyle, easy on the fretting hand all night long. Premium fretting work means the frent ends are a few milimiters from the edge of the fingerboard, give a lot of comfort and not chance of sharp frent ends due to humidity fluctiations.

*Full Specs:*

- Body: Cinamomo

- Top: Imbuia (Brazilian walnut)

- Neck: 5-piece louro pardo / maple

- Fingerboard: Brazilian rosewood (Jacarandá da Bahia)

- Scale: 32" | Spacing: 18mm | Strings: 5

- Premium fret installation & dressing

- Markings: Dots

- Bridge: NOVA

-String Holders: NOVA

-Strings: Magma Nickel

-Pickups: S Martyn Double coils humbuckers

-Preamp: S Martyn 3 Bands

-Volume: Push Pull active and Passive

-Blend, Treble, Mids, Bass, and Tone Control

-Switch: Mid frequency: 440hz/990hz

-Switch: 3 different pickup selections (Single outers and inners, double)

Bright, punchy attack with warm harmonic depth, slap-ready snap on top, fingerstyle bloom underneath.


You can check our IG @BASSNSOULGEAR page for some videos to be posted soon of this bass

If you have any general question, please send me a message.

item.title: Fender USA VINTAGE Musicmaster Bass '81 Black/R — $1,634.26
item.description:
$1,634.26 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

Carefully selected consigned items (sales on your behalf)

The Musicmaster Bass is part of the lineage of student models produced by Fender. While inheriting the design philosophy of the higher-end Precision Bass, it was introduced in 1970 as a single-pickup model with a more compact body and a 30-inch scale. Its streamlined design allows it to shine with a distinctive Fender sound and presence. It transcends the boundaries of an entry-level model and continues to be loved as a unique instrument.

This particular guitar, shipped around 1981, features a popular specification: a black ash body and rosewood fingerboard.

【body】
The total weight is approximately 3.8 kg. The body is made of ash. The black finish and black and chrome parts give it a sharp, rugged look. There are signs of aging in the paint, some discoloration of the rubber, scratches and dents, cracks in the pickguard, and tarnishing on the metal parts, giving it the feel of a vintage instrument. The strap pin at the end has been replaced.

【neck】
The neck is made of maple, and the fingerboard is made of rosewood. There is some discoloration of the topcoat on the headstock, some dents with paint peeling on the back of the neck, and some tarnishing on the metal parts, but overall it is in relatively good condition. The tuners are a little stiff to wind, but they function properly. The frets and nut show only slight wear, and there is some upward bowing on the neck, which can cause some buzzing on the higher frets depending on the picking technique, but this does not affect playability. The headstock thickness is 15.5mm. The nut width is 41.7mm. It features a sturdy neck shape.

[Electrical System]
The controls are a simple [1 volume/1 tone] configuration. The control system parts, including the pickups, have been kept in their original condition.

The ash body's attack and the rosewood fingerboard's moderate warmth coexist in perfect balance. Based on the compression and tight low end characteristic of a 30-inch scale, rolling off the tone knob produces a rounded, vintage-like bass, while opening it up brings out a bright, focused attack. Thanks to its simple circuit configuration, you can enjoy the instrument's true character straight and true.

Accessories: Fender-specific chipboard case (slightly damaged)

Weight: 3.8kg
Body Material: White Ash
Neck Material/Scale Length: Maple 1 piece/762mm (30inch)
Fingerboard/Fret Marker/Radius: Rosewood round laminate/Pearloid dot/184.15mm (7.25inch)
Nut Width/Fret Size: 41.7mm/2.2mm, 20 Fret
Neck Thickness (1F/7F/12F): 23.0mm/24.1mm/25.6mm
Tone Capacitor: Dielectron CircleD 100V
Pickup: Fender Singlecoil pickup for Musicmaster Bass (Stratocaster Style Flat polepiece), 5.75kΩ

Heartman Vintage Guitars is a specialty store for vintage guitars and basses operated by Ikebe Musical Instruments.

• Maple w/ dual Wenge laminate neck
• Pale Moon Ebony fingerboard
• 24 medium jumbo frets
• Dual Aguilar DCB passive pickups
• Black hardware throughout including Gotoh tuners
• Satin finish for a smooth, natural feel
• Includes Gator hard case

This bass is equally at home in gospel, fusion, progressive, jazz, and modern fingerstyle settings. The Aguilar DCB pickups provide a full, articulate voice with excellent string-to-string balance, while the neck feels fast and comfortable despite the extended range.

If you've been looking for a boutique six-string that stands out visually and sonically without the wait time of a new custom build, this is an excellent opportunity.

Feel free to reach out with any questions or for additional photos, videos, or sound clips.

item.title: ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle — $1,099
item.description:
$1,099 · Brand New · LV Guitars

ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle

The Surveyor '87 delivers a balanced, ergonomic platform with its thin U neck contour and moderate 9 lbs, 12 oz weight — comfortable for extended playing sessions without fatigue. The Rainbow Crackle finish commands visual presence while the bolt-on construction and classic proportions ground this instrument in proven bass fundamentals. Built around vintage-voiced electronics, this bass voices warm, articulate low end suitable for vintage-influenced and contemporary styles alike.

Playability & Build

The 34" scale neck pairs a thin U contour with Macassar ebony fingerboard and 21 XJ frets for smooth, fast navigation across the neck. Neck dimensions measure .760" at the 1st fret and .840" at the 12th fret, establishing a comfortable grip, while the 42mm molded nut and 400mm fingerboard radius support both fingerstyle and pick-driven approaches.

Tone & Electronics

The Seymour Duncan Vintage P and Vintage Jazz pickup pairing delivers warm, full-bodied tone with articulate high-end clarity — classic voicing that cuts through without harshness. Passive electronics with Volume/Balance/Tone controls and Gotoh 201-B4 bridge provide reliable hardware and straightforward tone-shaping, while D'Addario XL165 strings (.045/.065/.085/.105) arrive pre-installed.

Key Features
  • Bolt-on alder body with maple neck and Macassar ebony fingerboard
  • 34" scale length with 42mm molded nut
  • Thin U neck contour; .760" at 1st fret, .840" at 12th fret
  • 21 XJ frets with 400mm fingerboard radius
  • Rainbow Crackle finish with black hardware
  • Seymour Duncan Vintage P (neck) and Vintage Jazz (bridge) pickups
  • Passive electronics: Volume/Balance/Tone layout
  • Gotoh 201-B4 bridge and LTD Vintage tuners
  • D'Addario XL165 strings pre-installed (.045/.065/.085/.105)
  • 9 lbs, 12 oz total weight

The Surveyor '87 combines ergonomic design, vintage-style electronics, and straightforward hardware into a versatile platform suited to players seeking authentic tone and reliable playability.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Custom 4 Lusithand NFP loaded — $650
item.description:
$650 · Very Good · Brian's Used Equipment

Schecter Stiletto Custom 4 made in Korea modified with a Lusithand double NFP MkII preamp.

This preamp is very similar to a Wal/Alembic type preamp with a parametric filter for each pickup, pickup blend, and master volume. Tonally, there is an inherent aggressive character as it’s a bit higher gain than the original EMG preamp, but a lot more tonal variety between pickups can be achieved with the filters and blend working together.

This bass also originally had painted gold hardware, but most has been changed to black besides the strap buttons. The individual bridges are the same type as the OEM bridges but black. Original tuners were cheap Gotoh machine heads but have since been changed with Hipshot Ultralites with mini clover keys. Knobs are aluminum sourced from Lusithand.

item.title: Warwick Thumb BO 6 2023 — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Excellent · Matthew's Gear Outlet

I’m sad to see this one go but wrist issues and a need for money has left me no choice. The bass is a 2023 Thumb Bolt on German Pro Series Six String. It’s in excellent condition and has recently been waxed and PLEK’d. I purchased it new in January 2026. The bass has no wear and plays like a dream. It comes with a Warwick Gig Bag and a can of Warwick surface finisher. $2,500 – buyer pays shipping costs.

item.title: Kay K-5915 Speed Demon Bass - Sunburst — $1,600
item.description:
$1,600 · Very Good · Benny’s Guitars

Beautiful ‘66 vintage Kay K-5915 Speed Demon semi-hollow body bass Guitar. I am the third owner and the gentleman I purchased it from bought the bass back in 1967 and has taken very good care of it. It is fully functional, the electronics work and it sounds gorgeous. I had the neck straightened at Guitar Center, and the intonation is very good on it. Very slight buzzing on the high end of the G string, but can be adjusted to player taste using bridge adjuster. Has the famous speed bump pickup that produces that unique, vibey, thumpy sound that vintage Kay basses from this era are known for. The checkerboard binding is in pristine condition. Comes with original chipboard case. If there was ever a beautiful specimen of a K-5915, this is it! This is your chance to own this piece of Kay history. The bass does have some blemishes and discoloration in the finish that is expected for a 59-year-old bass.

item.title: Kala PSGR-FS U-Bass Passenger Fretted Mahogany Ukulele Bass 2010s - Satin Natural — $150
item.description:
$150 · Very Good · Keep Portland Geared

Kala’s popular U-Bass ukulele-sized bass is made of mahogany with a satin finish and comes equipped with black die-cast tuners, Aquila Thundergut strings and a Kala UK-500B preamp with EQ and built-in tuner.

The Passenger model adds a white binding and rosette for stylish ornamentation. The Journeyman sets itself apart with a cutaway body shape and f-holes, and retailed at $299.99.

This one is in great condition with no major nicks or dings - see the photos for condition details. I noticed that the G-string output was a little lower than the other strings, but it pulled off its gigs well.

I mainly used this to record indoor live streams during COVID and so it was played indoors. The original strings were worn out and replaced with Kala replacements. These new strings do not have much play on them and are good to go.

Also comes with original gig bag as shipped from Kala (double backpack straps make it easy to transport).

Kala Passenger Acoustic-Electric U-Bass Specs:
Top: Mahogany
Back & Sides: Mahogany
Binding: Cream
Finish: Satin
Neck: Mahogany
Nut: Plastic
Saddle: Uk-500B Piezo Pickup
Headstock: Standard
Strings: Aquila Thundergut®
Tuners: Custom Black Die Cast
Electronics: Uk-500B
Short Scale: 20.875 in
Overall Length: 29.625 in
Includes: Padded Gig Bag

item.description:

Here is a beautiful looking American Fender Jazz Bass in excellent condition .Pick up replaced with lundgren pick up (kept original pick up). Having them rewound . The Lundgren pups sound great. the tone pot was replaced . Comes with tweed Fender case in great condition.Comes with original bridge chrome cover. Bass weighs around 10 lbs .
